{{Short description|Factor by which time, length, and relativistic mass change at high velocities}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Lorentz factor
| image = Lorentz factor.svg
| caption = The Lorentz factor <math>\gamma</math> as a function of velocity. It diverges as <math>v \to c</math>.
| topics = [[Special relativity]], [[Speed of light]], [[Time dilation]], [[Length contraction]], [[Mass–energy equivalence]]
| formula = <math>\gamma = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 - v^2/c^2}}</math>
}}

The '''Lorentz factor''' (or '''Lorentz term''') is a quantity that expresses how much measurements of [[time]], [[length]], and other physical properties change for an object while it moves. It appears in the equations of [[special relativity]] and is named after the Dutch physicist [[Hendrik Lorentz]].

:<math>\gamma = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 - v^2/c^2}}</math>

where:
* <math>v</math> is the relative velocity between inertial reference frames
* <math>c</math> is the [[speed of light]] in vacuum

== Properties ==
* <math>\gamma \geq 1</math> for all <math>v < c</math>
* <math>\gamma = 1</math> when <math>v = 0</math> (rest frame)
* <math>\gamma \to \infty</math> as <math>v \to c</math>
* For low velocities: <math>\gamma \approx 1 + \frac{v^2}{2c^2}</math> (second-order approximation)

== Key relativistic effects using <math>\gamma</math> ==

=== Time dilation ===
:<math>\Delta t' = \gamma \Delta t</math>
A moving clock runs slower by factor <math>\gamma</math>.

=== Length contraction ===
:<math>L = \frac{L_0}{\gamma}</math>
A moving object is contracted in the direction of motion.

=== Relativistic mass ===
:<math>m = \gamma m_0</math>
The [[relativistic mass]] increases with velocity.

=== Relativistic energy and momentum ===
:<math>E = \gamma m_0 c^2</math>
:<math>p = \gamma m_0 v</math>

The [[energy–momentum relation]]:
:<math>E^2 = (pc)^2 + (m_0 c^2)^2</math>

== Values at common speeds ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! <math>v/c</math> !! <math>\gamma</math> !! Application
|-
| 0 || 1.000 || At rest
|-
| 0.1 || 1.005 || Chemical reactions (~1% of c)
|-
| 0.866 || 2.000 || Significant time dilation
|-
| 0.99 || 7.09 || Cosmic ray muons
|-
| 0.9999 || 70.7 || [[Large Hadron Collider]] protons
|-
| 0.999999991 || ~7,500 || [[Oh-My-God particle]]
|}

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Length contraction]]
* [[Lorentz transformation]]
* [[Special relativity]]
* [[Speed of light]]
* [[Time dilation]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Special relativity]]
[[Category:Equations of physics]]
